articles.headline: NASA-ISRO NISAR Satellite Successfully Launched, Marks New Era in Earth Observation and Climate Monitoring
articles.explainer:
• The NASA-ISRO Synthetic Aperture Radar (NISAR) satellite, a joint project between the US and Indian space agencies, was successfully launched into orbit.
• It is the world's first dual-frequency (L-band and S-band) radar imaging satellite, designed for comprehensive remote sensing.
• NISAR will provide crucial data for understanding and managing climate change, natural disasters, ecosystems, and Earth's dynamic processes.
• The mission highlights robust space collaboration between India and the United States, advancing global scientific research.
• Data from NISAR will be vital for applications in agriculture, forest mapping, glaciology, and disaster response.
gsSubTopics: Space technology
Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Environmental science
Disaster management
Bilateral relations
prelimsPoints: NISAR is a joint mission of NASA (USA) and ISRO (India).
It is the first dual-frequency (L-band and S-band) radar imaging satellite.
Its primary purpose is Earth observation for climate change, disasters, and ecosystems.
The satellite uses SAR technology for high-resolution images irrespective of weather conditions.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of international collaborations in space technology, citing NISAR as an example.
Analyze the potential applications of NISAR data in addressing climate change challenges and improving disaster management in India.
Evaluate India's growing capabilities in space technology and its role in global scientific endeavors.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of the NISAR satellite orbiting Earth, with NASA and ISRO logos.
articles.trivia: NISAR's data will be used to measure changes in Earth's land and ice surfaces down to a centimeter, helping to understand processes like glacier retreat and earthquake deformation.

articles.headline: Ministry of Culture Announces Repatriation of Ancient Piprahwa Gems from UK, Reinforcing Cultural Heritage Rights
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Culture confirmed the successful repatriation of ancient Piprahwa gems, which were taken from India during British colonial rule.
• The artefacts, previously subject to an attempted auction by Sotheby's in May, will now return to India.
• This move signifies a major victory for India's ongoing efforts to reclaim its stolen cultural heritage from abroad.
• The Piprahwa relics are historically significant, believed to be associated with Buddhist traditions and early Indian civilization.
• The repatriation underscores the importance of cultural diplomacy and international cooperation in protecting heritage.
gsSubTopics: Indian Heritage and Culture
Modern Indian History (British Rule)
Post-independence consolidation and reorganization within the country
Cultural diplomacy
prelimsPoints: Piprahwa is an archaeological site in Uttar Pradesh, India, known for early Buddhist relics.
The gems are believed to be part of the relics discovered in the Piprahwa stupa.
Repatriation efforts are led by the Ministry of Culture, Government of India.
Sotheby's attempted to auction these items, leading to intervention.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ethical and legal complexities surrounding the restitution of cultural artifacts taken during colonial periods.
Analyze the significance of such repatriations for India's cultural identity and national pride.
Examine the role of cultural diplomacy in strengthening international relations and resolving historical injustices.
articles.imageDescription: Ancient gems and artifacts discovered at the Piprahwa archaeological site.
articles.trivia: The Piprahwa stupa is considered one of the earliest Buddhist stupas, and some scholars believe the relics found there might contain actual remains of Buddha.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: History
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Ancient Indian History and Art Forms

articles.headline: US Imposes 25% Tariffs on Indian Exports Amidst Geopolitical Tensions Over Russian Oil Purchases
articles.explainer:
• US President Donald Trump announced a 25% tariff on certain Indian exports to the United States.
• The tariffs are primarily attributed to India's continued purchases of oil from Russia, a move seen by the US as undermining sanctions.
• This decision is expected to significantly impact India-US trade relations and put pressure on India's energy diplomacy.
• India has consistently defended its oil purchases from Russia, citing national energy security interests and favourable pricing.
• The tariffs may lead to a re-evaluation of India's trade strategy and diversification of export markets.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighbourhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
prelimsPoints: US President Donald Trump imposed a 25% tariff on Indian exports.
The primary reason cited is India's purchases of Russian oil.
This move affects India-US trade relations.
India maintains its stance on energy security and sourcing oil from Russia.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of US tariffs on India's economic growth and its strategy for diversifying export markets.
Discuss the challenges India faces in balancing its strategic autonomy and energy security with pressures from major global powers.
Evaluate the future trajectory of India-US bilateral relations in light of these trade disputes and geopolitical realignments.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and the United States, with trade charts in the background.
articles.trivia: India became one of the largest buyers of Russian crude oil after the Ukraine conflict, benefiting from discounted prices.

articles.headline: Vietnamese Automaker VinFast Begins Production at New US$500 Million Factory in Thoothukudi, Tamil Nadu
articles.explainer:
• Vietnamese electric vehicle manufacturer VinFast officially commenced production at its new facility in Thoothukudi, Tamil Nadu.
• The factory represents an investment of US$500 million and is projected to create over 3,000 direct and indirect jobs.
• This strategic move aligns with India's 'Make in India' and 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiatives, boosting local manufacturing capabilities.
• The plant is expected to contribute significantly to Tamil Nadu's industrial growth and position India as a hub for EV manufacturing.
• VinFast's entry signals increasing foreign direct investment (FDI) interest in India's rapidly expanding electric vehicle market.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Industrial policy
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Investment models
prelimsPoints: Vietnamese automaker VinFast started production in Thoothukudi, Tamil Nadu.
The investment for the factory is US$500 million.
It aims to create over 3,000 jobs.
The company focuses on electric vehicle manufacturing.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of foreign direct investment in boosting India's manufacturing sector and achieving 'Make in India' objectives.
Analyze the potential of Tamil Nadu as an investment destination for the automotive industry, particularly in the EV segment.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities for India in becoming a global hub for electric vehicle production.
articles.imageDescription: A VinFast electric car on an assembly line inside a factory.
articles.trivia: Thoothukudi, also known as Tuticorin, is a major port city in Tamil Nadu and an industrial hub, strategically located for exports.

articles.headline: India Reviews Progress on G20 Leaders' Declaration Commitments, Focus on Global South Agenda
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of External Affairs conducted a high-level review of the implementation of commitments made during India's G20 Presidency.
• Emphasis was placed on advancing the agenda for the Global South, including sustainable development goals and climate finance.
• Discussions focused on the progress of initiatives related to digital public infrastructure, green development, and women-led development.
• India reiterated its commitment to working with the G20 Troika (Brazil, India, South Africa) to ensure continuity and impact of the G20 agenda.
• The review highlighted India's continued leadership in shaping global economic and developmental discourse.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India reviewed commitments from its G20 Presidency.
Key focus areas include Global South agenda, SDGs, climate finance, and digital public infrastructure.
India is part of the G20 Troika along with Brazil and South Africa (for 2025).
The review was conducted by the Ministry of External Affairs.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's role in advocating for the interests of the Global South within multilateral forums like the G20.
Discuss the achievements and challenges in implementing the G20 Leaders' Declaration, particularly in areas like climate finance and sustainable development.
Evaluate the significance of digital public infrastructure as a model for global development, championed by India.
articles.imageDescription: G20 logo with flags of member nations, emphasizing India's leadership.
articles.trivia: The G20 comprises 19 countries and the European Union, representing around two-thirds of the world's population, 85% of global GDP, and 75% of global trade.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Directs Stricter Scrutiny for Environmental Clearances, Emphasizes Public Participation
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India issued a landmark ruling, mandating stricter scrutiny for environmental clearances (ECs) of development projects.
• The ruling emphasizes the critical importance of public participation in Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) processes, calling for enhanced and meaningful public hearings.
• This decision aims to bolster environmental protection and prevent ecological degradation by ensuring transparency and accountability in project approvals.
• The Court highlighted the need to balance developmental aspirations with the imperative of preserving ecological balance and community rights.
• The judgment is expected to have far-reaching implications for infrastructure and industrial projects across the country.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
Environmental pollution and degradation, Environmental Impact Assessment
prelimsPoints: The Supreme Court mandated stricter scrutiny for Environmental Clearances (ECs).
It emphasized public participation in Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) processes.
The ruling aims to prevent ecological degradation.
The decision reinforces the balance between development and environment.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of the judiciary in upholding environmental governance and balancing developmental needs with ecological preservation.
Analyze the challenges in ensuring meaningful public participation in EIA processes and suggest measures for improvement.
Evaluate the potential impact of this Supreme Court ruling on India's infrastructure development and industrial projects.
articles.imageDescription: A gavel resting on legal documents, with a blurred image of a green forest in the background.
articles.trivia: The concept of 'sustainable development' was first popularized by the Brundtland Commission Report (Our Common Future) in 1987, defining it as development that meets the needs of the present without compromising the ability of future generations to meet their own needs.

articles.headline: Special NIA Court Acquits Former MP Pragya Singh Thakur and Five Others in 2006 Malegaon Bombings Case
articles.explainer:
• A special National Investigation Agency (NIA) court acquitted former Member of Parliament Pragya Singh Thakur and five co-accused in the 2006 Malegaon bombings case.
• The court cited insufficient evidence to prove their involvement beyond reasonable doubt, leading to their discharge.
• The Malegaon bombings, which occurred in 2006, claimed several lives and injured many, becoming a high-profile terror case.
• The acquittal raises questions regarding the investigative processes and the challenges in prosecuting complex terror-related cases.
• This verdict has significant legal and political implications, rekindling debates on judicial fairness and investigative agency efficacy.
gsSubTopics: Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Role of civil services in a democracy
prelimsPoints: A special NIA court acquitted Pragya Singh Thakur and five others in the 2006 Malegaon bombings case.
The reason for acquittal was insufficient evidence.
The case involved multiple blasts in Malegaon, Maharashtra.
NIA is the central agency for investigating terror-related cases in India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges faced by investigative agencies in prosecuting terror-related cases in India and the importance of due process.
Analyze the implications of such acquittals on public trust in the justice system and the efficacy of anti-terror laws.
Evaluate the role of the judiciary in ensuring fair trial and protecting individual liberties in cases involving national security.
articles.imageDescription: A courtroom scene with a judge's bench and legal documents.
articles.trivia: The National Investigation Agency (NIA) was constituted under the National Investigation Agency Act, 2008, following the 26/11 Mumbai terror attacks.

articles.headline: Prajwal Revanna Convicted of Rape by Bengaluru Special Court, Sentenced to Rigorous Life Imprisonment
articles.explainer:
• Former MP Prajwal Revanna was convicted of rape by a special court in Bengaluru today, following a high-profile sexual assault case.
• He was subsequently sentenced to rigorous life imprisonment, marking a significant verdict in cases involving public figures.
• The conviction underscores the judiciary's firm stance against sexual violence and the pursuit of justice for victims.
• The case garnered national attention, highlighting issues of accountability for those in positions of power and the challenges victims face.
• This judgment reinforces the legal framework for protection of women and the importance of robust judicial processes.
gsSubTopics: Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary
Mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: Prajwal Revanna, a former MP, was convicted of rape by a special court in Bengaluru.
He was sentenced to rigorous life imprisonment.
The case highlights legal action against sexual violence.
Special courts are designated for specific types of cases.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of the judiciary in ensuring justice for victims of sexual violence, especially when perpetrators are influential public figures.
Analyze the impact of such convictions on political ethics and the accountability of elected representatives in India.
Evaluate the effectiveness of existing laws and mechanisms for the protection of women from sexual offenses in the country.
articles.imageDescription: Symbolic image of justice scales, with handcuffs in the foreground.
articles.trivia: Special courts are often established to fast-track trials for specific categories of offenses or individuals, such as cases against MPs/MLAs, or cases under specific acts like POCSO or NIA.

articles.headline: Tragic Bus Accident Claims 18 Lives on Kanwar Yatra Pilgrimage in Deoghar, Jharkhand
articles.explainer:
• At least 18 pilgrims on the annual Kanwar Yatra were killed in a tragic bus accident in Deoghar, Jharkhand.
• The incident occurred as the bus, carrying devotees, overturned on a highway, leading to multiple fatalities and injuries.
• The state government has announced ex-gratia payments for the victims' families and initiated an investigation into the cause of the accident.
• The event highlights persistent concerns regarding road safety, particularly during large-scale pilgrimages and public gatherings.
• Authorities are reviewing safety protocols for the Kanwar Yatra to prevent future such tragedies.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of Indian Society
Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
Disaster and disaster management
prelimsPoints: 18 people died in a bus accident during the Kanwar Yatra.
The incident occurred in Deoghar, Jharkhand.
Kanwar Yatra is an annual pilgrimage of devotees of Lord Shiva.
State government announced ex-gratia for victims' families.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges in ensuring safety and crowd management during large-scale religious pilgrimages in India.
Analyze the factors contributing to road accidents in India and suggest comprehensive measures for improving road safety.
Evaluate the role of state disaster management authorities in responding to and preventing such tragedies.
articles.imageDescription: Emergency services at the site of a bus accident, with debris on the road.
articles.trivia: The Kanwar Yatra is an annual pilgrimage of devotees of Lord Shiva, who collect holy water from the Ganges (or other sacred rivers) and carry it in 'kanwars' to various Shiva temples.

articles.headline: Protests Erupt Over Alleged Irregularities in SSC Exams and Quality of Conducting Agency Eduquity Career Technologies
articles.explainer:
• Widespread protests erupted across several cities, with aspirants alleging poor conduct and irregularities in Staff Selection Commission (SSC) examinations.
• Students raised concerns over the inferior quality of services provided by Eduquity Career Technologies, the private agency responsible for conducting the exams.
• Allegations include technical glitches, unfair evaluation practices, and lack of transparency in the recruitment process.
• The protests highlight persistent issues in public sector recruitment, affecting the career prospects of millions of youth.
• Demands include a thorough investigation into Eduquity, re-conduct of affected examinations, and greater transparency in selection procedures.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Transparency & Accountability
prelimsPoints: Protests are against alleged irregularities in Staff Selection Commission (SSC) exams.
Concerns were raised about Eduquity Career Technologies, the conducting agency.
Allegations include technical glitches and unfair evaluation.
SSC conducts examinations for various Group 'B' and 'C' posts in central government departments.
mainsPoints: Analyze the systemic issues contributing to irregularities in public sector recruitment examinations in India.
Discuss the impact of such irregularities on the aspirations of youth and public trust in government institutions.
Suggest reforms to enhance transparency, fairness, and efficiency in the conduct of competitive examinations by agencies like SSC.
articles.imageDescription: Students protesting with placards outside an examination body office.
articles.trivia: The Staff Selection Commission (SSC) is an organization under the Government of India to recruit staff for various posts in the various Ministries and Departments of the Government of India and in Subordinate Offices.

articles.headline: Defence Ministry Unveils New Policy to Boost Indigenous Production of Critical Military Hardware
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence launched a comprehensive new policy aimed at significantly boosting indigenous production of critical military hardware.
• The policy focuses on achieving 'Aatmanirbhar Bharat' (self-reliant India) in the defence sector, reducing reliance on foreign imports.
• It introduces new incentives for private sector participation, including MSMEs and startups, in defence manufacturing and R&D.
• Emphasizes the development of indigenous defence corridors and technology transfer mechanisms.
• The initiative is expected to enhance national security capabilities and stimulate economic growth through local job creation.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Defence launched a new policy for indigenous defence production.
The policy aims for 'Aatmanirbhar Bharat' in defence.
It includes incentives for private sector, MSMEs, and startups.
Focus areas are defence corridors and technology transfer.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic imperative of achieving self-reliance in defence manufacturing for India's national security.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities for the private sector, including MSMEs, in contributing to India's defence industrial base.
Evaluate the economic benefits and geopolitical implications of strengthening indigenous defence production capabilities.
articles.imageDescription: Advanced military aircraft being manufactured in an Indian defence factory.
articles.trivia: India is one of the largest importers of defence equipment globally, making the 'Aatmanirbhar Bharat' initiative critical for reducing this dependency.

articles.headline: Union Government Launches 'Gram Swasthya Seva Yojana' to Strengthen Primary Healthcare in Rural India
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government officially launched the 'Gram Swasthya Seva Yojana', a new flagship scheme aimed at bolstering primary healthcare services in rural areas.
• The scheme focuses on preventive and promotive health, integrating digital health infrastructure and tele-consultation services.
• It includes extensive training and deployment of community health workers and 'Swasthya Mitras' to bridge the healthcare gap.
• The Yojana seeks to address health disparities in remote regions and move towards universal health coverage.
• Funding mechanisms involve a combination of central and state contributions, emphasizing a collaborative approach.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: The scheme 'Gram Swasthya Seva Yojana' focuses on rural primary healthcare.
It integrates digital health and tele-consultation.
It involves training and deployment of community health workers.
The objective is to achieve universal health coverage in rural areas.
mainsPoints: Discuss the critical challenges faced by primary healthcare in rural India and how the 'Gram Swasthya Seva Yojana' aims to address them.
Analyze the potential of digital health technologies in transforming healthcare delivery in remote and underserved areas.
Evaluate the importance of community health workers and 'Swasthya Mitras' in achieving universal health coverage and improving public health outcomes.
articles.imageDescription: A rural health worker interacting with villagers in a remote Indian village.
articles.trivia: India's National Health Policy 2017 aims to achieve the highest possible level of health and well-being for all through a preventive and promotive healthcare orientation.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Easing Inflationary Pressures and Global Uncertainties
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) announced its decision to maintain the status quo on the repo rate.
• The decision comes amidst signs of easing inflationary pressures domestically, balanced against lingering global economic uncertainties.
• The RBI emphasized its commitment to supporting economic growth while keeping inflation within the target band.
• Analysts suggest the move reflects a cautious approach, allowing previous rate hikes to fully transmit through the economy.
• The decision will influence lending rates, credit availability, and overall economic activity in the coming months.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary Policy
prelimsPoints: RBI's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) kept the repo rate unchanged.
The decision considers easing domestic inflation and global uncertainties.
RBI's primary mandate is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
The repo rate is the rate at which the RBI lends money to commercial banks.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of the Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee in managing inflation and fostering economic growth in India.
Analyze the factors that influence the MPC's decisions on interest rates, considering both domestic and global economic indicators.
Evaluate the impact of monetary policy decisions on various sectors of the Indian economy, including lending, investment, and consumption.
articles.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India building, with currency notes in the foreground.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) in India has six members – three officials from the RBI and three external experts nominated by the Government of India.

articles.headline: India and France Hold High-Level Strategic Dialogue, Reinforce Cooperation in Indo-Pacific and Defence
articles.explainer:
• India and France concluded a high-level strategic dialogue, reaffirming their commitment to deepening cooperation across various sectors.
• The discussions focused on enhancing strategic partnership, particularly in the Indo-Pacific region, defence, and space collaboration.
• Both nations reiterated their shared vision for a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, countering regional challenges.
• Agreements were reached to boost joint defence production, technology transfer, and counter-terrorism efforts.
• The dialogue underscores the growing convergence of interests between India and France as key strategic partners on the global stage.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighbourhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: India and France held a high-level strategic dialogue.
Key areas of cooperation include Indo-Pacific, defence, and space.
Both countries share a vision for a free and open Indo-Pacific.
Agreements focused on joint defence production and technology transfer.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the India-France strategic partnership in the evolving geopolitical landscape, particularly in the Indo-Pacific region.
Discuss the various pillars of cooperation between India and France, including defence, space, and counter-terrorism.
Evaluate how this bilateral relationship contributes to India's strategic autonomy and its foreign policy objectives.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and France waving together, symbolizing bilateral cooperation.
articles.trivia: India and France have a long-standing strategic partnership, established in 1998, covering cooperation in nuclear energy, space, and defence.

articles.headline: Ministry of Environment Launches 'Amrit Dharohar' Initiative to Protect and Revive Critical Wetlands
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change launched 'Amrit Dharohar', a new national initiative focused on the conservation and sustainable use of wetlands.
• The program aims to protect and revive critical Ramsar sites and other ecologically significant wetlands across India.
• It emphasizes community participation, traditional knowledge, and nature-based solutions for wetland management.
• 'Amrit Dharohar' seeks to enhance biodiversity, mitigate climate change impacts, and secure the ecological services provided by wetlands.
• The initiative will involve capacity building, public awareness campaigns, and inter-sectoral collaboration for effective conservation.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity
Climate change
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest & Climate Change
prelimsPoints: 'Amrit Dharohar' is an initiative by the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change.
Its focus is on the conservation and sustainable use of wetlands, including Ramsar sites.
The program promotes community participation and traditional knowledge.
Objectives include enhancing biodiversity and mitigating climate change.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological importance of wetlands and the threats they face in India.
Analyze how the 'Amrit Dharohar' initiative can contribute to the conservation and sustainable management of wetlands, particularly Ramsar sites.
Evaluate the role of community participation and traditional knowledge in successful environmental conservation programs.
articles.imageDescription: A serene wetland landscape with migratory birds and lush vegetation.
articles.trivia: India is a signatory to the Ramsar Convention on Wetlands, an intergovernmental treaty that provides the framework for national action and international cooperation for the conservation and wise use of wetlands and their resources.

articles.headline: India Accelerates Rollout of Digital Public Infrastructure to Boost Financial Inclusion and E-Governance
articles.explainer:
• India announced an accelerated rollout plan for its Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) to further enhance financial inclusion and e-governance services.
• The expansion focuses on increasing the reach and adoption of platforms like UPI, Aadhaar, and DigiLocker in rural and underserved areas.
• The initiative aims to streamline public service delivery, reduce corruption, and provide equitable access to digital services for all citizens.
• India's DPI model is being recognized globally as a blueprint for other developing nations seeking digital transformation.
• The government is also investing in digital literacy programs to ensure broader participation and maximize the benefits of DPI.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
E-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it.
prelimsPoints: India is accelerating the rollout of its Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI).
Key components include UPI, Aadhaar, and DigiLocker.
The aim is to enhance financial inclusion and e-governance, especially in rural areas.
India's DPI model is being studied by other developing countries.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) in fostering financial inclusion and good governance in India.
Analyze the challenges associated with the widespread adoption and equitable access to DPI, particularly in rural and marginalized communities.
Evaluate how India's DPI model can serve as a blueprint for other developing nations striving for digital transformation.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic representing interconnected digital services like UPI, Aadhaar, and DigiLocker.
articles.trivia: UPI (Unified Payments Interface) is a real-time payment system developed by the National Payments Corporation of India (NPCI) for instant bank-to-bank transactions.

highlights: NASA-ISRO NISAR Satellite Launched: Marks new era in Earth observation and climate monitoring, enhancing US-India space collaboration.
Piprahwa Gems Repatriated: Ministry of Culture successfully reclaims ancient Indian artifacts from the UK, underscoring cultural heritage rights.
US Imposes Tariffs on Indian Exports: US President Trump levies 25% tariffs citing India's Russian oil purchases, impacting bilateral trade.
VinFast Begins Production in Thoothukudi: Vietnamese automaker's US$500 million factory boosts Tamil Nadu's manufacturing and job creation.
India Reviews G20 Commitments: Focus on advancing Global South agenda, sustainable development, and digital public infrastructure.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the NASA-ISRO Synthetic Aperture Radar (NISAR) mission, consider the following statements:
1. It is the first dual-frequency (L-band and S-band) radar imaging satellite.
2. Its primary objective is to study the Earth's atmosphere and weather patterns.
3. It is a joint project between the space agencies of India and Russia.
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. NISAR is indeed the world's first dual-frequency (L-band and S-band) radar imaging satellite. Statement 2 is incorrect; while it contributes to understanding Earth systems, its primary objective is comprehensive remote sensing of Earth's land and ice surfaces, including climate change, natural disasters, and ecosystems, rather than just atmosphere and weather. Statement 3 is incorrect; NISAR is a joint project between NASA (USA) and ISRO (India), not Russia.

prelimsMocks.question: The 'Piprahwa gems', recently in news for repatriation efforts, are historically associated with which of the following?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Piprahwa gems are historically significant artifacts believed to be associated with early Buddhist traditions and were discovered in the Piprahwa stupa, one of the earliest Buddhist stupas, in Uttar Pradesh. Some scholars believe the relics found there might contain actual remains of Buddha.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following statements best describes the 'Amrit Dharohar' initiative?
options: (a) A scheme for promoting traditional Indian art forms.
(b) A program for the conservation and sustainable use of wetlands.
(c) An initiative to develop ancient Indian pilgrimage sites.
(d) A project for preserving historical monuments and archaeological sites.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) A program for the conservation and sustainable use of wetlands.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The 'Amrit Dharohar' initiative, launched by the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change, is focused on the conservation and sustainable use of wetlands, including critical Ramsar sites across India.

prelimsMocks.question: The Vietnamese automaker VinFast recently commenced production of electric vehicles in India. In which state has its factory been established?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Vietnamese electric vehicle manufacturer VinFast has officially commenced production at its new facility in Thoothukudi, Tamil Nadu, representing a significant investment and job creation.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following components of India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI):
1. Unified Payments Interface (UPI)
2. Aadhaar
3. DigiLocker
Which of the above are aimed at enhancing financial inclusion and e-governance?
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three, UPI (for digital payments and financial transactions), Aadhaar (for identity verification and service delivery), and DigiLocker (for digital document storage and verification), are key components of India's Digital Public Infrastructure aimed at boosting financial inclusion and e-governance services across the country.

mainsMocks.question: Analyze the strategic implications of the NASA-ISRO NISAR mission for India's space diplomacy and its role in global climate change mitigation efforts. (150 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Space technology; GS2: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce NISAR as a landmark US-India collaboration. Discuss its scientific capabilities for Earth observation, climate monitoring, and disaster management. Elaborate on how this joint mission strengthens India's standing in space technology and fosters international cooperation. Conclude by highlighting its contribution to global climate data and sustainable development.

mainsMocks.question: Discuss the challenges faced by India in balancing its energy security needs with geopolitical pressures, in light of the recent US tariffs on Indian exports. How can India navigate such complexities to safeguard its economic interests? (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests; GS3: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by outlining the context of US tariffs due to Russian oil purchases and India's stance on energy security. Analyze the dual challenge of maintaining affordable energy supply and navigating international sanctions. Suggest strategies such as diversifying energy sources, strengthening strategic partnerships (e.g., with Middle East, Africa), exploring alternative trade routes, boosting domestic production, and engaging in diplomatic dialogue to mitigate trade impacts.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's emphasis on stricter scrutiny for Environmental Clearances and greater public participation in EIA processes marks a significant step towards environmental governance. Critically examine the effectiveness of existing EIA processes in India and suggest reforms for more robust environmental protection. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Government policies and interventions; GS3: Environmental pollution and degradation, Environmental Impact Assessment.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the SC ruling and its significance. Briefly explain EIA and its objectives. Critically examine current EIA effectiveness, highlighting issues like inadequate public hearings, lack of independent assessment, post-clearance monitoring gaps, and political interference. Suggest reforms like mandatory independent expert committees, strengthening regulatory bodies, ensuring transparency in data, leveraging technology, and empowering local communities for meaningful participation.

quickBytes.text: NISAR is the first satellite to use two different radar frequencies (L-band and S-band).
The Piprahwa stupa, where the repatriated gems were found, is located in Uttar Pradesh.
The Kanwar Yatra is an annual pilgrimage dedicated to Lord Shiva, undertaken by devotees.
The National Investigation Agency (NIA) investigates and prosecutes terror-related offenses in India.
Thoothukudi (Tuticorin) in Tamil Nadu is a major port city and an industrial hub.
The G20 comprises 19 countries and the European Union, representing major global economies.
The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) has six members.
'Aatmanirbhar Bharat' in defence aims to reduce import dependency for military hardware.
The Ramsar Convention provides the framework for wetland conservation globally.
UPI, Aadhaar, and DigiLocker are key components of India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI).

GS1.headline: Ministry of Culture Announces Repatriation of Ancient Piprahwa Gems from UK, Reinforcing Cultural Heritage Rights
GS1.explainer:
• The Ministry of Culture confirmed the successful repatriation of ancient Piprahwa gems, which were taken from India during British colonial rule.
• The artefacts, previously subject to an attempted auction by Sotheby's in May, will now return to India.
• This move signifies a major victory for India's ongoing efforts to reclaim its stolen cultural heritage from abroad.
• The Piprahwa relics are historically significant, believed to be associated with Buddhist traditions and early Indian civilization.
• The repatriation underscores the importance of cultural diplomacy and international cooperation in protecting heritage.
gsSubTopics: Indian Heritage and Culture
Modern Indian History (British Rule)
Post-independence consolidation and reorganization within the country
Cultural diplomacy
prelimsPoints: Piprahwa is an archaeological site in Uttar Pradesh, India, known for early Buddhist relics.
The gems are believed to be part of the relics discovered in the Piprahwa stupa.
Repatriation efforts are led by the Ministry of Culture, Government of India.
Sotheby's attempted to auction these items, leading to intervention.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ethical and legal complexities surrounding the restitution of cultural artifacts taken during colonial periods.
Analyze the significance of such repatriations for India's cultural identity and national pride.
Examine the role of cultural diplomacy in strengthening international relations and resolving historical injustices.
GS1.imageDescription: Ancient gems and artifacts discovered at the Piprahwa archaeological site.
GS1.trivia: The Piprahwa stupa is considered one of the earliest Buddhist stupas, and some scholars believe the relics found there might contain actual remains of Buddha.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: History
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Ancient Indian History and Art Forms

GS1.headline: Tragic Bus Accident Claims 18 Lives on Kanwar Yatra Pilgrimage in Deoghar, Jharkhand
GS1.explainer:
• At least 18 pilgrims on the annual Kanwar Yatra were killed in a tragic bus accident in Deoghar, Jharkhand.
• The incident occurred as the bus, carrying devotees, overturned on a highway, leading to multiple fatalities and injuries.
• The state government has announced ex-gratia payments for the victims' families and initiated an investigation into the cause of the accident.
• The event highlights persistent concerns regarding road safety, particularly during large-scale pilgrimages and public gatherings.
• Authorities are reviewing safety protocols for the Kanwar Yatra to prevent future such tragedies.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of Indian Society
Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
Disaster and disaster management
prelimsPoints: 18 people died in a bus accident during the Kanwar Yatra.
The incident occurred in Deoghar, Jharkhand.
Kanwar Yatra is an annual pilgrimage of devotees of Lord Shiva.
State government announced ex-gratia for victims' families.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges in ensuring safety and crowd management during large-scale religious pilgrimages in India.
Analyze the factors contributing to road accidents in India and suggest comprehensive measures for improving road safety.
Evaluate the role of state disaster management authorities in responding to and preventing such tragedies.
GS1.imageDescription: Emergency services at the site of a bus accident, with debris on the road.
GS1.trivia: The Kanwar Yatra is an annual pilgrimage of devotees of Lord Shiva, who collect holy water from the Ganges (or other sacred rivers) and carry it in 'kanwars' to various Shiva temples.

GS2.headline: US Imposes 25% Tariffs on Indian Exports Amidst Geopolitical Tensions Over Russian Oil Purchases
GS2.explainer:
• US President Donald Trump announced a 25% tariff on certain Indian exports to the United States.
• The tariffs are primarily attributed to India's continued purchases of oil from Russia, a move seen by the US as undermining sanctions.
• This decision is expected to significantly impact India-US trade relations and put pressure on India's energy diplomacy.
• India has consistently defended its oil purchases from Russia, citing national energy security interests and favourable pricing.
• The tariffs may lead to a re-evaluation of India's trade strategy and diversification of export markets.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighbourhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
prelimsPoints: US President Donald Trump imposed a 25% tariff on Indian exports.
The primary reason cited is India's purchases of Russian oil.
This move affects India-US trade relations.
India maintains its stance on energy security and sourcing oil from Russia.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of US tariffs on India's economic growth and its strategy for diversifying export markets.
Discuss the challenges India faces in balancing its strategic autonomy and energy security with pressures from major global powers.
Evaluate the future trajectory of India-US bilateral relations in light of these trade disputes and geopolitical realignments.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and the United States, with trade charts in the background.
GS2.trivia: India became one of the largest buyers of Russian crude oil after the Ukraine conflict, benefiting from discounted prices.

GS2.headline: India Reviews Progress on G20 Leaders' Declaration Commitments, Focus on Global South Agenda
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of External Affairs conducted a high-level review of the implementation of commitments made during India's G20 Presidency.
• Emphasis was placed on advancing the agenda for the Global South, including sustainable development goals and climate finance.
• Discussions focused on the progress of initiatives related to digital public infrastructure, green development, and women-led development.
• India reiterated its commitment to working with the G20 Troika (Brazil, India, South Africa) to ensure continuity and impact of the G20 agenda.
• The review highlighted India's continued leadership in shaping global economic and developmental discourse.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India reviewed commitments from its G20 Presidency.
Key focus areas include Global South agenda, SDGs, climate finance, and digital public infrastructure.
India is part of the G20 Troika along with Brazil and South Africa (for 2025).
The review was conducted by the Ministry of External Affairs.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's role in advocating for the interests of the Global South within multilateral forums like the G20.
Discuss the achievements and challenges in implementing the G20 Leaders' Declaration, particularly in areas like climate finance and sustainable development.
Evaluate the significance of digital public infrastructure as a model for global development, championed by India.
GS2.imageDescription: G20 logo with flags of member nations, emphasizing India's leadership.
GS2.trivia: The G20 comprises 19 countries and the European Union, representing around two-thirds of the world's population, 85% of global GDP, and 75% of global trade.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Directs Stricter Scrutiny for Environmental Clearances, Emphasizes Public Participation
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India issued a landmark ruling, mandating stricter scrutiny for environmental clearances (ECs) of development projects.
• The ruling emphasizes the critical importance of public participation in Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) processes, calling for enhanced and meaningful public hearings.
• This decision aims to bolster environmental protection and prevent ecological degradation by ensuring transparency and accountability in project approvals.
• The Court highlighted the need to balance developmental aspirations with the imperative of preserving ecological balance and community rights.
• The judgment is expected to have far-reaching implications for infrastructure and industrial projects across the country.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
Environmental pollution and degradation, Environmental Impact Assessment
prelimsPoints: The Supreme Court mandated stricter scrutiny for Environmental Clearances (ECs).
It emphasized public participation in Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) processes.
The ruling aims to prevent ecological degradation.
The decision reinforces the balance between development and environment.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of the judiciary in upholding environmental governance and balancing developmental needs with ecological preservation.
Analyze the challenges in ensuring meaningful public participation in EIA processes and suggest measures for improvement.
Evaluate the potential impact of this Supreme Court ruling on India's infrastructure development and industrial projects.
GS2.imageDescription: A gavel resting on legal documents, with a blurred image of a green forest in the background.
GS2.trivia: The concept of 'sustainable development' was first popularized by the Brundtland Commission Report (Our Common Future) in 1987, defining it as development that meets the needs of the present without compromising the ability of future generations to meet their own needs.

GS2.headline: Special NIA Court Acquits Former MP Pragya Singh Thakur and Five Others in 2006 Malegaon Bombings Case
GS2.explainer:
• A special National Investigation Agency (NIA) court acquitted former Member of Parliament Pragya Singh Thakur and five co-accused in the 2006 Malegaon bombings case.
• The court cited insufficient evidence to prove their involvement beyond reasonable doubt, leading to their discharge.
• The Malegaon bombings, which occurred in 2006, claimed several lives and injured many, becoming a high-profile terror case.
• The acquittal raises questions regarding the investigative processes and the challenges in prosecuting complex terror-related cases.
• This verdict has significant legal and political implications, rekindling debates on judicial fairness and investigative agency efficacy.
gsSubTopics: Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Role of civil services in a democracy
prelimsPoints: A special NIA court acquitted Pragya Singh Thakur and five others in the 2006 Malegaon bombings case.
The reason for acquittal was insufficient evidence.
The case involved multiple blasts in Malegaon, Maharashtra.
NIA is the central agency for investigating terror-related cases in India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges faced by investigative agencies in prosecuting terror-related cases in India and the importance of due process.
Analyze the implications of such acquittals on public trust in the justice system and the efficacy of anti-terror laws.
Evaluate the role of the judiciary in ensuring fair trial and protecting individual liberties in cases involving national security.
GS2.imageDescription: A courtroom scene with a judge's bench and legal documents.
GS2.trivia: The National Investigation Agency (NIA) was constituted under the National Investigation Agency Act, 2008, following the 26/11 Mumbai terror attacks.

GS2.headline: Prajwal Revanna Convicted of Rape by Bengaluru Special Court, Sentenced to Rigorous Life Imprisonment
GS2.explainer:
• Former MP Prajwal Revanna was convicted of rape by a special court in Bengaluru today, following a high-profile sexual assault case.
• He was subsequently sentenced to rigorous life imprisonment, marking a significant verdict in cases involving public figures.
• The conviction underscores the judiciary's firm stance against sexual violence and the pursuit of justice for victims.
• The case garnered national attention, highlighting issues of accountability for those in positions of power and the challenges victims face.
• This judgment reinforces the legal framework for protection of women and the importance of robust judicial processes.
gsSubTopics: Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary
Mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: Prajwal Revanna, a former MP, was convicted of rape by a special court in Bengaluru.
He was sentenced to rigorous life imprisonment.
The case highlights legal action against sexual violence.
Special courts are designated for specific types of cases.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of the judiciary in ensuring justice for victims of sexual violence, especially when perpetrators are influential public figures.
Analyze the impact of such convictions on political ethics and the accountability of elected representatives in India.
Evaluate the effectiveness of existing laws and mechanisms for the protection of women from sexual offenses in the country.
GS2.imageDescription: Symbolic image of justice scales, with handcuffs in the foreground.
GS2.trivia: Special courts are often established to fast-track trials for specific categories of offenses or individuals, such as cases against MPs/MLAs, or cases under specific acts like POCSO or NIA.

GS2.headline: Protests Erupt Over Alleged Irregularities in SSC Exams and Quality of Conducting Agency Eduquity Career Technologies
GS2.explainer:
• Widespread protests erupted across several cities, with aspirants alleging poor conduct and irregularities in Staff Selection Commission (SSC) examinations.
• Students raised concerns over the inferior quality of services provided by Eduquity Career Technologies, the private agency responsible for conducting the exams.
• Allegations include technical glitches, unfair evaluation practices, and lack of transparency in the recruitment process.
• The protests highlight persistent issues in public sector recruitment, affecting the career prospects of millions of youth.
• Demands include a thorough investigation into Eduquity, re-conduct of affected examinations, and greater transparency in selection procedures.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Transparency & Accountability
prelimsPoints: Protests are against alleged irregularities in Staff Selection Commission (SSC) exams.
Concerns were raised about Eduquity Career Technologies, the conducting agency.
Allegations include technical glitches and unfair evaluation.
SSC conducts examinations for various Group 'B' and 'C' posts in central government departments.
mainsPoints: Analyze the systemic issues contributing to irregularities in public sector recruitment examinations in India.
Discuss the impact of such irregularities on the aspirations of youth and public trust in government institutions.
Suggest reforms to enhance transparency, fairness, and efficiency in the conduct of competitive examinations by agencies like SSC.
GS2.imageDescription: Students protesting with placards outside an examination body office.
GS2.trivia: The Staff Selection Commission (SSC) is an organization under the Government of India to recruit staff for various posts in the various Ministries and Departments of the Government of India and in Subordinate Offices.

GS2.headline: Union Government Launches 'Gram Swasthya Seva Yojana' to Strengthen Primary Healthcare in Rural India
GS2.explainer:
• The Union Government officially launched the 'Gram Swasthya Seva Yojana', a new flagship scheme aimed at bolstering primary healthcare services in rural areas.
• The scheme focuses on preventive and promotive health, integrating digital health infrastructure and tele-consultation services.
• It includes extensive training and deployment of community health workers and 'Swasthya Mitras' to bridge the healthcare gap.
• The Yojana seeks to address health disparities in remote regions and move towards universal health coverage.
• Funding mechanisms involve a combination of central and state contributions, emphasizing a collaborative approach.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: The scheme 'Gram Swasthya Seva Yojana' focuses on rural primary healthcare.
It integrates digital health and tele-consultation.
It involves training and deployment of community health workers.
The objective is to achieve universal health coverage in rural areas.
mainsPoints: Discuss the critical challenges faced by primary healthcare in rural India and how the 'Gram Swasthya Seva Yojana' aims to address them.
Analyze the potential of digital health technologies in transforming healthcare delivery in remote and underserved areas.
Evaluate the importance of community health workers and 'Swasthya Mitras' in achieving universal health coverage and improving public health outcomes.
GS2.imageDescription: A rural health worker interacting with villagers in a remote Indian village.
GS2.trivia: India's National Health Policy 2017 aims to achieve the highest possible level of health and well-being for all through a preventive and promotive healthcare orientation.

GS2.headline: India and France Hold High-Level Strategic Dialogue, Reinforce Cooperation in Indo-Pacific and Defence
GS2.explainer:
• India and France concluded a high-level strategic dialogue, reaffirming their commitment to deepening cooperation across various sectors.
• The discussions focused on enhancing strategic partnership, particularly in the Indo-Pacific region, defence, and space collaboration.
• Both nations reiterated their shared vision for a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, countering regional challenges.
• Agreements were reached to boost joint defence production, technology transfer, and counter-terrorism efforts.
• The dialogue underscores the growing convergence of interests between India and France as key strategic partners on the global stage.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighbourhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: India and France held a high-level strategic dialogue.
Key areas of cooperation include Indo-Pacific, defence, and space.
Both countries share a vision for a free and open Indo-Pacific.
Agreements focused on joint defence production and technology transfer.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the India-France strategic partnership in the evolving geopolitical landscape, particularly in the Indo-Pacific region.
Discuss the various pillars of cooperation between India and France, including defence, space, and counter-terrorism.
Evaluate how this bilateral relationship contributes to India's strategic autonomy and its foreign policy objectives.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and France waving together, symbolizing bilateral cooperation.
GS2.trivia: India and France have a long-standing strategic partnership, established in 1998, covering cooperation in nuclear energy, space, and defence.

GS2.headline: India Accelerates Rollout of Digital Public Infrastructure to Boost Financial Inclusion and E-Governance
GS2.explainer:
• India announced an accelerated rollout plan for its Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) to further enhance financial inclusion and e-governance services.
• The expansion focuses on increasing the reach and adoption of platforms like UPI, Aadhaar, and DigiLocker in rural and underserved areas.
• The initiative aims to streamline public service delivery, reduce corruption, and provide equitable access to digital services for all citizens.
• India's DPI model is being recognized globally as a blueprint for other developing nations seeking digital transformation.
• The government is also investing in digital literacy programs to ensure broader participation and maximize the benefits of DPI.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
E-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it.
prelimsPoints: India is accelerating the rollout of its Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI).
Key components include UPI, Aadhaar, and DigiLocker.
The aim is to enhance financial inclusion and e-governance, especially in rural areas.
India's DPI model is being studied by other developing countries.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) in fostering financial inclusion and good governance in India.
Analyze the challenges associated with the widespread adoption and equitable access to DPI, particularly in rural and marginalized communities.
Evaluate how India's DPI model can serve as a blueprint for other developing nations striving for digital transformation.
GS2.imageDescription: A graphic representing interconnected digital services like UPI, Aadhaar, and DigiLocker.
GS2.trivia: UPI (Unified Payments Interface) is a real-time payment system developed by the National Payments Corporation of India (NPCI) for instant bank-to-bank transactions.

GS3.headline: NASA-ISRO NISAR Satellite Successfully Launched, Marks New Era in Earth Observation and Climate Monitoring
GS3.explainer:
• The NASA-ISRO Synthetic Aperture Radar (NISAR) satellite, a joint project between the US and Indian space agencies, was successfully launched into orbit.
• It is the world's first dual-frequency (L-band and S-band) radar imaging satellite, designed for comprehensive remote sensing.
• NISAR will provide crucial data for understanding and managing climate change, natural disasters, ecosystems, and Earth's dynamic processes.
• The mission highlights robust space collaboration between India and the United States, advancing global scientific research.
• Data from NISAR will be vital for applications in agriculture, forest mapping, glaciology, and disaster response.
gsSubTopics: Space technology
Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Environmental science
Disaster management
Bilateral relations
prelimsPoints: NISAR is a joint mission of NASA (USA) and ISRO (India).
It is the first dual-frequency (L-band and S-band) radar imaging satellite.
Its primary purpose is Earth observation for climate change, disasters, and ecosystems.
The satellite uses SAR technology for high-resolution images irrespective of weather conditions.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of international collaborations in space technology, citing NISAR as an example.
Analyze the potential applications of NISAR data in addressing climate change challenges and improving disaster management in India.
Evaluate India's growing capabilities in space technology and its role in global scientific endeavors.
GS3.imageDescription: Illustration of the NISAR satellite orbiting Earth, with NASA and ISRO logos.
GS3.trivia: NISAR's data will be used to measure changes in Earth's land and ice surfaces down to a centimeter, helping to understand processes like glacier retreat and earthquake deformation.

GS3.headline: Vietnamese Automaker VinFast Begins Production at New US$500 Million Factory in Thoothukudi, Tamil Nadu
GS3.explainer:
• Vietnamese electric vehicle manufacturer VinFast officially commenced production at its new facility in Thoothukudi, Tamil Nadu.
• The factory represents an investment of US$500 million and is projected to create over 3,000 direct and indirect jobs.
• This strategic move aligns with India's 'Make in India' and 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiatives, boosting local manufacturing capabilities.
• The plant is expected to contribute significantly to Tamil Nadu's industrial growth and position India as a hub for EV manufacturing.
• VinFast's entry signals increasing foreign direct investment (FDI) interest in India's rapidly expanding electric vehicle market.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Industrial policy
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Investment models
prelimsPoints: Vietnamese automaker VinFast started production in Thoothukudi, Tamil Nadu.
The investment for the factory is US$500 million.
It aims to create over 3,000 jobs.
The company focuses on electric vehicle manufacturing.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of foreign direct investment in boosting India's manufacturing sector and achieving 'Make in India' objectives.
Analyze the potential of Tamil Nadu as an investment destination for the automotive industry, particularly in the EV segment.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities for India in becoming a global hub for electric vehicle production.
GS3.imageDescription: A VinFast electric car on an assembly line inside a factory.
GS3.trivia: Thoothukudi, also known as Tuticorin, is a major port city in Tamil Nadu and an industrial hub, strategically located for exports.

GS3.headline: Defence Ministry Unveils New Policy to Boost Indigenous Production of Critical Military Hardware
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence launched a comprehensive new policy aimed at significantly boosting indigenous production of critical military hardware.
• The policy focuses on achieving 'Aatmanirbhar Bharat' (self-reliant India) in the defence sector, reducing reliance on foreign imports.
• It introduces new incentives for private sector participation, including MSMEs and startups, in defence manufacturing and R&D.
• Emphasizes the development of indigenous defence corridors and technology transfer mechanisms.
• The initiative is expected to enhance national security capabilities and stimulate economic growth through local job creation.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Defence launched a new policy for indigenous defence production.
The policy aims for 'Aatmanirbhar Bharat' in defence.
It includes incentives for private sector, MSMEs, and startups.
Focus areas are defence corridors and technology transfer.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic imperative of achieving self-reliance in defence manufacturing for India's national security.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities for the private sector, including MSMEs, in contributing to India's defence industrial base.
Evaluate the economic benefits and geopolitical implications of strengthening indigenous defence production capabilities.
GS3.imageDescription: Advanced military aircraft being manufactured in an Indian defence factory.
GS3.trivia: India is one of the largest importers of defence equipment globally, making the 'Aatmanirbhar Bharat' initiative critical for reducing this dependency.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Easing Inflationary Pressures and Global Uncertainties
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) announced its decision to maintain the status quo on the repo rate.
• The decision comes amidst signs of easing inflationary pressures domestically, balanced against lingering global economic uncertainties.
• The RBI emphasized its commitment to supporting economic growth while keeping inflation within the target band.
• Analysts suggest the move reflects a cautious approach, allowing previous rate hikes to fully transmit through the economy.
• The decision will influence lending rates, credit availability, and overall economic activity in the coming months.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary Policy
prelimsPoints: RBI's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) kept the repo rate unchanged.
The decision considers easing domestic inflation and global uncertainties.
RBI's primary mandate is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
The repo rate is the rate at which the RBI lends money to commercial banks.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of the Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee in managing inflation and fostering economic growth in India.
Analyze the factors that influence the MPC's decisions on interest rates, considering both domestic and global economic indicators.
Evaluate the impact of monetary policy decisions on various sectors of the Indian economy, including lending, investment, and consumption.
GS3.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India building, with currency notes in the foreground.
GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) in India has six members – three officials from the RBI and three external experts nominated by the Government of India.

GS3.headline: Ministry of Environment Launches 'Amrit Dharohar' Initiative to Protect and Revive Critical Wetlands
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change launched 'Amrit Dharohar', a new national initiative focused on the conservation and sustainable use of wetlands.
• The program aims to protect and revive critical Ramsar sites and other ecologically significant wetlands across India.
• It emphasizes community participation, traditional knowledge, and nature-based solutions for wetland management.
• 'Amrit Dharohar' seeks to enhance biodiversity, mitigate climate change impacts, and secure the ecological services provided by wetlands.
• The initiative will involve capacity building, public awareness campaigns, and inter-sectoral collaboration for effective conservation.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity
Climate change
GS3.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest & Climate Change
prelimsPoints: 'Amrit Dharohar' is an initiative by the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change.
Its focus is on the conservation and sustainable use of wetlands, including Ramsar sites.
The program promotes community participation and traditional knowledge.
Objectives include enhancing biodiversity and mitigating climate change.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological importance of wetlands and the threats they face in India.
Analyze how the 'Amrit Dharohar' initiative can contribute to the conservation and sustainable management of wetlands, particularly Ramsar sites.
Evaluate the role of community participation and traditional knowledge in successful environmental conservation programs.
GS3.imageDescription: A serene wetland landscape with migratory birds and lush vegetation.
GS3.trivia: India is a signatory to the Ramsar Convention on Wetlands, an intergovernmental treaty that provides the framework for national action and international cooperation for the conservation and wise use of wetlands and their resources.

sources: Ministry of Environment, Forest & Climate Change
